headlines.title: Israel didn't drag US into war with Iran, Netanyahu says
headlines.summary: Israel's prime minister says the war with Iran will "take as long as necessary", as Tehran vows to act with "zero restraint" if attacks on its infrastructure continue.

headlines.title: Are US and Israel aligned on Iran war? Deciphering Trump's post after gas field attacks
headlines.summary: What does Trump's Truth Social post after gas field attacks tell us about US-Israeli alignment?

headlines.title: DR Congo and Rwanda agree to ease tensions after talks in US
headlines.summary: The conflict has persisted despite a peace deal signed by DR Congo and Rwanda in December.

headlines.title: Former counter-terrorism head investigated by FBI over alleged leaks
headlines.summary: The former counter-terrorism head resigned on Tuesday over the war with Iran, saying the country posed "no imminent threat" to the US.

headlines.title: How Europe sleepwalked into yet another energy crisis
headlines.summary: It is not the first time that there has been deep energy-linked frustration in the heart of Europe.

headlines.title: Spain's king welcomes Mexico's World Cup invite after 'abuse' comments
headlines.summary: News of the invitation emerges after the monarch acknowledged "a lot of abuse" during the Spanish conquest of Mexico.

headlines.title: Norway crown princess to address Epstein links as son's rape trial closes
headlines.summary: Mette-Marit gives a much-anticipated interview, due to be broadcast on Friday.

headlines.title: Kevin Spacey and accusers settle before civil trial
headlines.summary: The actor agrees an out-of-court settlement with three men who accused him of sexual assault.

headlines.title: Chad warns Sudan it will retaliate after drone strike on mourners kills 17
headlines.summary: President Idris Mahamat Déby orders the military to be on high alert and a "total closure" of the border.

headlines.title: Iran conflict looms large over Trump's meeting with Japan PM
headlines.summary: The meeting with Sanae Takaichi comes days after Trump criticised Japan for not answering his demands for help in the Strait of Hormuz.

headlines.title: Three Palestinian women killed in beauty salon during Iranian missile attack
headlines.summary: Others were injured when the salon in the southern West Bank was hit, while a Thai worker was killed by falling shrapnel in Israel.

headlines.title: Cyprus leader calls for frank discussion on 'colonial' UK bases
headlines.summary: The UK's two military bases on Cyprus are a "colonial consequence" on the island, says President Nikos Christodoulides.

headlines.title: South Africans say criminal gangs are exploiting the water crisis
headlines.summary: Residents of some areas have been without mains water for weeks.

headlines.title: The Iran war is causing a global energy crisis - can China withstand it?
headlines.summary: With oil supply disrupted, Beijing's oil reserves and renewable energy push are being put to the test.

headlines.title: Afghan evacuees in limbo in Qatar camp accuse US of betrayal
headlines.summary: More than 1,100 Afghans evacuated by the US fear their route to promised resettlement is closed for good.

headlines.title: 'Very difficult to stop': BBC visits scene of Iran cluster bomb strike on Israel
headlines.summary: An elderly couple was killed after a bomb flew into their apartment in central Israel and exploded.

headlines.title: India's young are more educated than ever. So why are so many jobless?
headlines.summary: India's educational enrolment has surged but jobs, especially good ones, are not keeping pace, a new report finds.

headlines.title: Indonesia detains four soldiers over acid attack on activist
headlines.summary: Andrie Yunus, who criticised the army's role in government, suffered burns to 20% of his body.

headlines.title: Huge queues for jabs as meningitis vaccination scheme expanded
headlines.summary: Some queueing for vaccines at the University of Kent were turned away on Thursday.

headlines.title: Reports of children as young as 11 abused in West Midlands mini-marts, BBC reveals
headlines.summary: Child sexual abuse reports go back 10 years and were seen by safeguarding group which included police

headlines.title: Tories only party with a plan, says Badenoch as she launches election campaign
headlines.summary: Speaking ahead of the 7 May poll, the Tory leader says her party is the only one "that can actually fix things".

headlines.title: Reform vows to cut Scottish income tax below UK rate
headlines.summary: Nigel Farage's party has launched a manifesto and unveiled its candidates for the Holyrood election.

headlines.title: King opens world's longest coastal path around England
headlines.summary: The King Charles coastal path will allow walkers right of access to the entire coast for the first time.

headlines.title: Bank ready to raise interest rates if Iran war price 'shock' persists
headlines.summary: Policymakers vote unanimously to hold rates at 3.75% after the Iran war prompts a reversal in the debate over borrowing costs.

headlines.title: Patients harmed as Covid pandemic brought NHS close to collapse, inquiry finds
headlines.summary: Third report into the pandemic says patients and staff were failed as health service only just coped.

headlines.title: Hospital order for man who stalked Myleene Klass
headlines.summary: Peter Windsor is sentenced after sending items including an air pistol to Klass and another presenter.

headlines.title: Badenoch backs Tory MP in row over Muslim prayer event
headlines.summary: The Tory leader has faced calls to sack a colleague who called public prayers an "act of domination".

headlines.title: UK reveals aid priorities after major cuts to budget
headlines.summary: Aid groups and MPs have raised concerns over the move to prioritise defence spending over aid.

headlines.title: Two men in court accused of spying on London Jewish community for Iran
headlines.summary: The prosecution alleges they carried out reconnaissance of targets including Britain's oldest synagogue.

headlines.title: 'Designer' dog owners report more problem behaviours, vets warn
headlines.summary: The Royal Veterinary College says popular "doodle" dogs do not always behave as expected.

headlines.title: UK does not 'agree with Trump on every issue' - Cooper
headlines.summary: Foreign Secretary Yvette Cooper has hit back at President Trump's criticism of the UK response to the conflict in Iran.
